changelog.summary: Initial launch — welcome content, getting started guides, and sample blog posts
Site rebrand: renamed to cameronians.scot with new tagline, green theme color, and an added category

pages.title: Welcome to LocalCMS
pages.content:
Hello and welcome! 👋

This is LocalCMS — a fully serverless, browser-based content management system. Everything is stored right here in your browser, so it works offline, costs nothing to host, and keeps your content completely private.

What you can do

  • Create and edit pages with a rich-text editor
  • Organise content into categories
  • Upload and embed images from your device
  • Publish pages or save them as drafts
  • Customise the site name, tagline and password

To open the admin panel, click the 🔒 icon in the top-right corner. The default password is admin1234 — change it in Settings once you're in.

Your content, your browser, your rules.

pages.title: Getting Started in 5 Minutes
pages.content:
Set up your site quickly

LocalCMS is designed to get you writing in under five minutes. Here's the fast path.

1 · Customise your site

Go to Settings in the admin panel and update your site name and tagline. They appear on the homepage and browser tab.

2 · Set up categories

Head to Categories and create the topics that work for you — Blog, News, Projects, Recipes, anything. Categories appear as nav links once they have at least one published page.

3 · Write your first page

Click Pages → New Page, give it a title, pick a category, write your content using the editor toolbar, and hit Save.

4 · Upload images

Go to Media to upload images from your device. They're stored in your browser and can be inserted into any page editor with the 🖼️ button.

5 · Submit for publish

When you're happy with your changes, go to Submit, add a brief change summary, and send your edits to the site admin. They'll publish the update, and visitors will see it on their next visit — automatically.

pages.title: How the Publish Workflow Works
pages.content:
Local edits → admin publish → live for everyone

LocalCMS uses a simple two-step workflow that keeps content quality high while keeping the infrastructure cost at zero.

Step 1 — Edit locally

The CMS admin panel runs entirely in your browser. Create pages, upload images, rearrange categories — all without touching a server. Your changes are saved in localStorage as you go, so you can close the tab and come back later without losing anything.

Step 2 — Submit to admin

When you're ready to go live, open the Submit section in the admin panel. Write a brief summary of what changed, then send it to your admin — by email, or as a ready-to-upload file.

Step 3 — Admin publishes

The site's whole content lives in a single content.json file. The admin simply replaces that file on the server — via FTP, a Git commit, or any other upload method — and bumps nothing else. Once it's live, the new version is published.

Step 4 — Visitors get updated automatically

The app checks for a newer published version on every visit. If the version in content.json is newer than what's stored locally, it refreshes the content automatically — visitors always see the latest, no cache-clearing needed.

Think of it as a pull-request workflow for your content — propose changes, get them reviewed, publish when ready.

pages.title: Rich-Text Editor Tips
pages.content:
Getting the most from the editor

The built-in editor handles everything you need for well-formatted pages. Here are the tricks worth knowing.

Keyboard shortcuts

  • Ctrl+B (Cmd+B on Mac) — Bold
  • Ctrl+I — Italic
  • Ctrl+U — Underline

The toolbar

  • H2 / H3 — Format the selected line as a section or sub-section heading
  • ¶ — Return a heading back to normal paragraph text
  • • List / 1. List — Start a bullet or numbered list
  • ❝ — Format the selected text as a pull-quote
  • 🔗 — Wrap selected text in a hyperlink
  • 🖼️ — Insert an image from your Media Library at the cursor position

Inserting images

Upload your images in the Media section first. Then in the editor, position your cursor where you want the image, and click 🖼️. A picker will show all your uploaded images.

Tip: keep images under 500 KB each — localStorage has a ~5 MB limit shared across all your content.

pages.title: Roadmap & Upcoming Features
pages.content:
What's coming next

LocalCMS is actively evolving. Here's a snapshot of what's on the roadmap.

Planned

  • Export / Import — Download your entire site as a JSON file and restore it on any device
  • Page ordering — Drag pages to reorder them in the navigation
  • Tags — Multi-tag pages for richer filtering
  • Search — Full-text search across all published content
  • Custom themes — Choose from a set of colour schemes for the public site

Under consideration

  • Scheduled publishing (publish at a future date)
  • Simple analytics (page views, stored locally)
  • Comments (visitor-submitted, stored locally)

Have a feature request? Use the Submit section in the admin to send it through with your next content update.
